detection_opportunities.signal: Off-hours service account logon from unknown source IP
detection_opportunities.detection_rule: Service account logon outside business hours (09:00-18:00) from IP not in baseline
detection_opportunities.analyst_action_required: Investigate svc_backup$ logon source 10.0.4.47 — verify if scheduled task or authorized change
detection_opportunities.containment_action: Disable svc_backup$ account, force password reset, investigate 10.0.4.47

detection_opportunities.signal: SMB subnet sweep from clinical server + Domain Admin enumeration on DC
detection_opportunities.detection_rule: 5 outbound SMB connections in 12s from single host with no baseline SMB history; net user /domain on DC from service account
detection_opportunities.analyst_action_required: Immediate — isolate AIIMS-PATIENT-MGMT-01, disable svc_backup$ across all systems, audit DC for scheduled tasks
detection_opportunities.containment_action: Network isolate PATIENT-MGMT-01, remove malicious scheduled task 'MicrosoftEdgeUpdateTaskMachineUA', disable svc_backup$
forensic_pivots: Check who created scheduled task MicrosoftEdgeUpdateTaskMachineUA on AIIMS-DC-01
Analyze base64 payload in task arguments — decodes to download from 185.220.101.47
Review all admin$ share accesses in past 48h

detection_opportunities.signal: 47GB archive written to Windows\Temp at midnight + VSS deletion
detection_opportunities.detection_rule: 7z.exe spawned by non-standard parent + bulk write >1GB to Temp directory + vssadmin delete shadows
detection_opportunities.analyst_action_required: CRITICAL — 47GB patient data staged for exfil, shadow copies deleted, immediate IR team response required
detection_opportunities.containment_action: Isolate AIIMS-DC-01, preserve forensic image of C:\Windows\Temp\~logs\, block all outbound from DC, incident declared

detection_opportunities.signal: Cobalt Strike C2 beacon to Tor exit node via typosquat domain
detection_opportunities.detection_rule: DNS query for typosquat domain resolving to known Tor exit node + periodic HTTPS from DC with JA3 matching Cobalt Strike + 2.3GB exfil
detection_opportunities.analyst_action_required: MANDATORY ESCALATION — active Cobalt Strike C2, data exfiltration confirmed, ransomware deployment imminent
detection_opportunities.containment_action: Full network isolation of all compromised hosts, emergency AD recovery, block 185.220.101.47 at perimeter, notify CERT-In under NCIIPC mandate

ttps_used.description: svc_backup$ credentials used for initial access — likely obtained via credential stuffing or password spray against AIIMS VPN/backup management portal
SMB sweep from AIIMS-PATIENT-MGMT-01, lateral movement to AIIMS-DC-01 using harvested credentials
net user /domain + whoami /all executed on DC for full domain enumeration
Malicious scheduled task 'MicrosoftEdgeUpdateTaskMachineUA' created on DC for persistence with base64 PowerShell payload

ttps_used.technique_name: Archive Collected Data: Archive via Utility
ttps_used.description: 7z.exe used to create 47.3GB password-protected archive of patient records in C:\Windows\Temp\~logs\
vssadmin delete shadows /all /quiet on Day 8; bcdedit recovery disable and WBEngine service stopped on Day 22
Cobalt Strike HTTPS beacon to 185.220.101.47 (Tor exit) via update.microsofft.com typosquat
2.3GB patient data exfiltrated via HTTPS POST to Cobalt Strike C2 in 50MB chunks
284,712 files encrypted with AES-256-CTR + RSA-4096 key wrapping, '.AIIMS_LOCKED' extension appended
All 5 domain admin accounts simultaneously locked via automated bad-password spray from DC

likely_actor.notes: TTPs consistent with APT41 playbook: dual espionage (data exfiltration) + financial extortion (ransomware), Cobalt Strike usage, Tor exit node C2, healthcare sector targeting
overlapping_iocs: 185.220.101.47 used in 3 prior APT41-attributed incidents (CERT-In CI-21-2024)
Cobalt Strike JA3 hash a0e9f5d64349fb13191bc781f81f42e1 seen in APT41 India-targeted campaigns Q3 2022
.AIIMS_LOCKED extension customization indicates targeted, not commodity, operation

services_disrupted: Online patient registration (completely offline for 15 days)
Hospital Information System — patient record access
impact.patient_care_impact: Critical — AIIMS returned to paper-based records for 15 days, elective surgeries cancelled, emergency cases handled manually

retroactive_detection_summary.outcome_if_detected_day_1: Attacker stopped at initial access — svc_backup$ disabled, no lateral movement, no data exfiltration, no ransomware
retroactive_detection_summary.outcome_if_detected_day_3: Persistence mechanism removed, lateral movement stopped, 47GB staging never occurs, domain not compromised
retroactive_detection_summary.outcome_if_detected_day_12: C2 severed, exfiltration blocked, 10 days to full remediation before ransomware deadline
retroactive_detection_summary.outcome_actual_day_22: Full ransomware deployment, 284,712 patient records encrypted, 15-day clinical disruption, $6.5M ransom demand
